summary: Young British Artist from Margate. Known for My Bed (disheveled bed installation), Everyone I Have Ever Slept With 1963-1995 (tent with appliqued names), neon text works, autobiographical blankets. Screamed outside Edvard Munch's home for a performance.

works.name: My Bed (1998)
works.description: Disheveled, unmade bed installation with cigarette packets, slippers, bloody underwear, condoms, empty liquor bottles on blue rug. Inspired by four days consuming nothing but alcohol. Jumped on by Mad for Real duo at Tate Britain.

clues.clue: Disheveled unmade bed with cigarette packets, slippers, bloody underwear, condoms; inspired by four days of only alcohol; jumped on by Mad for Real (Yuan Chai and Jian Jun Xi)
examples: Cigarette packets and a discarded pair of slippers were included in an installation by this artist, inspired by a four day period in which she consumed nothing but alcohol
Cai Yuan and Jian Jun Xi jumped on this Tracey Emin piece while shirtless

works.name: Everyone I Have Ever Slept With 1963-1995
works.description: Blue tent with names of everyone the artist had ever slept with appliqued inside, including Billy Childish. Inspired by the Shell Grotto in her hometown of Margate. Destroyed in 2004 warehouse fire.

clues.clue: Blue tent with names of everyone she'd ever slept with appliqued inside, including Billy Childish; inspired by Margate Shell Grotto; destroyed in 2004 warehouse fire
examples: The Shell Grotto in this artist's hometown of Margate inspired the design of a tent on which she appliqued the names of 'everyone [she] had ever slept with'
In 2004, property of this artist, which included a beach hut co-owned with Sarah Lucas, was destroyed in a warehouse fire

images.caption: Everyone I Have Ever Slept With 1963-1995

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: From Margate. Performance piece: screaming outside Edvard Munch's home (Homage to Edvard Munch and All My Dead Children). Neon text works (I Can Feel You Everywhere, The Kiss Was Beautiful). I've Got It All: Polaroid of money spewing from splayed legs. Autobiographical blankets. Chose to hang Egon Schiele works next to hers at Leopold Museum.

clues.clue: Performance: screaming in fetal position outside Edvard Munch's home
examples: A performance piece by this artist saw them incessantly scream while lying in the foetal position outside the home of Edvard Munch

clues.clue: Neon text works installed in various locations
examples: A Tracey Emin work in this medium that reads, 'I Can Feel You Everywhere,' is installed above a mantle in Kris Jenner's home

clues.clue: I've Got It All: Polaroid of money spewing from splayed legs
examples: portrayed money spewing out of her splayed legs in a Polaroid shot titled I've Got It All

clues.clue: Chose to hang Egon Schiele works next to hers at Leopold Museum
examples: Tracey Emin chose to hang several of this artist's works next to hers for an exhibition at the Leopold Museum

comprehensive_summary:
Tracey Emin is a Young British Artist from Margate, England, best known for provocative autobiographical works. My Bed (1998) is a disheveled, unmade bed installation with cigarette packets, slippers, bloody underwear, condoms, and empty liquor bottles, inspired by four days consuming only alcohol. The Chinese duo Mad for Real (Yuan Chai and Jian Jun Xi) jumped onto it at the Tate Britain.

Everyone I Have Ever Slept With 1963-1995 is a blue tent with names appliqued inside, including Billy Childish. Inspired by the Shell Grotto in Margate, it was destroyed in a 2004 warehouse fire (along with a beach hut co-owned with Sarah Lucas).

She performed Homage to Edvard Munch and All My Dead Children by screaming in the fetal position outside Munch's home. She creates neon text works and I've Got It All, a Polaroid of money spewing from her splayed legs. She creates autobiographical blankets and chose to hang Egon Schiele's works alongside hers at the Leopold Museum.
